doc_title: Securing Approval for Sponsorship of Conferences

steps.text: Provide CDC with documentation of a plan with a timeline for substantive involvement in, and development of, the content of the meeting.
Provide a written process of how presentations or abstracts are to be reviewed and criteria that will be used for selecting presentations.
Agree to abide by CDC Guiding Principles for the Conduct of Scientific Conferences, when applicable.
Establish a written process for approving conference funding.
Complete a co-sponsorship agreement with the non-federal co-sponsor.
Obtain approval by the Director, or his/her designee, of the CC/CO/NC(s) providing the funds.
Obtain CDC approval if CDC is to be identified as a sponsor of the event (for CDC grantees using general program funds).
Include the specified language (Reference 7D) in all program announcements when funds are to be awarded.
Submit a formal request in writing to the CCs/COs/NCs having related programmatic responsibility and/or the Office of the Director (OD), CDC.
Provide the required information (organization's mission, purpose, capacity, proposed content/format, intended audience, other cosponsors).
Review and approve or disapprove all requests submitted to the CC/CO/NC.
Coordinate requests made to the OD, CDC, promptly with the director(s) of the CCs/COs/NC(s) having programmatic responsibility.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating disapproval of the request.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating tentative approval containing specific language.
Review the full final conference agenda and other conference information.
Provide a written response to the conference organizer(s) indicating final approval or disapproval of sponsorship.
Complete a co-sponsorship agreement with the non-federal co-sponsor.
Assure that the proposed meeting facilities comply with ADAAG.
Obtain written certification of ADA compliance from facility management, or ensure the CC/CO/NC completes the Accessible Meetings Checklist.
Maintain a copy of all written correspondence regarding conferences on file in the Office of the Director of the CC/CO/NC.
Assign a specific person responsibility for maintaining these records and submitting a quarterly line listing to the OD, CDC.

preconditions.text: When CDC is asked to sponsor a conference with a nonfederal organization and funds are provided
When a conference is proposed post-award
When sponsorship of a conference is requested and does not involve expenditure of CDC funds
When the CC/CO/NC or the OD, CDC, does not wish to sponsor the conference
If the CC/CO/NC is interested in sponsoring the conference
After reviewing the full final conference agenda and other conference information
Prior to approving any request or issuing any obligation documents

constraints.text: No verbal disapproval of sponsorship may be provided.
No verbal approval of sponsorship may be provided.
All conferences sponsored by CDC shall be held in facilities that are fully accessible to the public as required by American with Disabilities Act Accessibility Guidelines (ADAAG).
CDC shall not co-sponsor an event with an entity that will not make information concerning funding publicly available.
